headline: Agent production infrastructure dominated the quarter as evaluation, persistence, and context emerged as the differentiating concerns.
body: The quarter centered on moving AI agents from prototype to production, with talks on continuous evaluation pipelines, online versus offline evals, and drift detection at scale [10], and on scaling a government-facing agent product through core loop harnesses, long context handling, and observability [2]. A recurring theme was persistence: durable session logs proposed as the core agent primitive [5], and coding agents critiqued for lacking memory across interactions [3]. On the modeling side, DeepReinforce released the Ornith family of open agentic coding models spanning 9B to 397B parameters with self-learned scaffolding for planning and retries [0], and recursive language models applied inference-time compute to coding reliability [6]. Breakouts skewed toward lower-level techniques including feed-forward networks, optical flow, RAFT, and primitive-action decomposition, none yet anchored to the window's items.

cited_items.title: Ornith (35B,9B) + Hermes,Zed: THE FULLY PRIVATE LOCAL AGENT is ACTUALLY HERE!
Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
